## DeepSeek V4 API Model Names (2026)

Based on the search results, DeepSeek V4 was officially released as a **preview version on April 24, 2026**. The API provides two model names:

### API Model Identifiers

| Model Name | Description | Parameters |
|---|---|---|
| `deepseek-v4-pro` | High-performance variant | 1.6T total, 49B active |
| `deepseek-v4-flash` | Lightweight, faster, cost-efficient | 284B total, 13B active |

### Key Details

- **Base URL**: `https://api.deepseek.com` (unchanged)
- **Context Length**: 1M tokens (million-token context)
- **Interfaces**: Supports both OpenAI ChatCompletions and Anthropic-compatible endpoints
- **Reasoning Modes**: Supports `non-thinking`, `thinking`, and `thinking_max` (via `reasoning_effort` parameter)
- **Recommended Settings**: `temperature=1.0`, `top_p=1.0`

### Legacy Deprecation

The older model names `deepseek-chat` and `deepseek-reasoner` are being **discontinued on July 24, 2026**. During the transition period, they automatically point to `deepseek-v4-flash` (in non-thinking and thinking modes respectively).

For a quick migration, simply change your `model` parameter:

```
# Old (legacy)
model="deepseek-chat"     →  model="deepseek-v4-pro"
model="deepseek-reasoner" →  model="deepseek-v4-pro"
```

## DeepSeek V4 vs V3.2 — Pricing & Token Cost Comparison (2026)

DeepSeek released **V4-Pro** and **V4-Flash** on April 24, 2026. Here's how the pricing breaks down:

---

### 📊 Direct Pricing Comparison (USD per million tokens)

| Model | Input ($/M tokens) | Output ($/M tokens) | Context Window |
|---|---|---|---|
| **DeepSeek V3.2** (previous gen) | $0.28 | $0.42 | 128K |
| **DeepSeek V4-Flash** | **$0.14** ↓50% | **$0.28** ↓33% | **1M** (8x larger) |
| **DeepSeek V4-Pro** | $1.74 | $3.48 | 1M |

*(Sources: [Simon Willison](https://simonwillison.net/2026/apr/24/deepseek-v4/), [ChosunBiz](https://prod.chosunbiz.com/en/en-international/2026/02/12/TPTTYRUQNNERLD6EDO7D6HPNVQ/))*

---

### 🔑 Key Takeaways

**1. V4-Flash: Direct replacement for V3.2 — cheaper with 8x more context**
- Flash directly replaces V3.2 (`deepseek-chat`) with **no price increase**—actually a **decrease**
- Cache miss input: **$1.00** → down **50%** from V3.2's $2.00
- Output: **$2.00** → down **33%** from V3.2's $3.00
- Context expanded from **128K → 1M** (8x more context at a lower price)
- Old model names `deepseek-chat` and `deepseek-reasoner` deprecated July 24, 2026

**2. V4-Pro: Premium tier with massive efficiency gains**
- Brand new high-end tier — output price is **8x** that of V3.2
- But massive efficiency improvements offset this:
  - At 1M context: **only 27%** of V3.2's single-token FLOPs
  - KV cache is only **10%** of V3.2
- Still the **cheapest frontier-class model** vs GPT-5.4 ($2.50/$15), Claude Sonnet 4.6 ($3/$15), Claude Opus 4.7 ($5/$25)
- **Limited throughput** currently due to high-end compute constraints
- **Major price cuts expected H2 2026** when 950 Ascend super nodes come online

**3. V4-Flash is the cheapest small model on the market**
- Beats even OpenAI's GPT-5.4 Nano ($0.20/$1.25)
- ~99% cheaper than Claude Opus 4.7

---

|---|---|---|
| **DeepSeek V4 Flash** | **$0.14** | **$0.28** |
| GPT-5.4 Nano | $0.20 | $1.25 |

| Claude Sonnet 4.6 | $3.00 | $15.00 |
| Claude Opus 4.7 | $5.00 | $25.00 |
| GPT-5.5 | $5.00 | $30.00 |

---

### 🧠 Why the price drop? Efficiency gains

DeepSeek's new **hybrid attention mechanism** (Compressed Sparse Attention + DeepSeek Sparse Attention) enables dramatic efficiency at long contexts:

| Metric @ 1M context | V4-Pro vs V3.2 | V4-Flash vs V3.2 |
|---|---|---|
| Single-token FLOPs | **27%** | **10%** |
| KV cache size | **10%** | **7%** |

V4-Pro has **1.6T total parameters** (49B active) vs V3.2's ~685B total (37B active) — 2.4x more total parameters but only 32% more active parameters, making it much more efficient per token.

Here's a comprehensive summary of what I found regarding **cc-Switch / Claude Code switch model API configuration** tools:

---

## 🔀 Key Tools for Switching Claude Code Models

### 1. **CC Switch** (by farion1231) — Cross-platform Desktop App
A full-featured GUI + CLI tool for managing multiple AI coding CLI tools (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, OpenClaw).

- **Install**: `brew install --cask cc-switch` (macOS) or download from GitHub
- **Key Features**:
  - 50+ built-in provider presets (SiliconFlow, GLM, Kimi, DeepSeek, etc.)
  - One-click switching between providers
  - MCP management, prompt management, skill management
  - Proxy/failover/high availability support
  - Cloud sync via WebDAV
- **Config location**: Managed through the app UI or stored in config files under `~/.claude/`
- **Docs**: [GitHub - farion1231/cc-switch](https://github.com/farion1231/cc-switch)

---

### 2. **claude-code-switch (ccm)** — CLI Tool by foreveryh
A one-command switcher for Claude Code supporting multiple providers.

- **Install**: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/foreveryh/claude-code-switch/main/quick-install.sh | bash`
- **Usage**:
  ```bash
  ccm config            # Configure API keys
  ccm glm               # Switch to GLM
  ccm deepseek          # Switch to DeepSeek
  ccm kimi global       # Switch to Kimi global
  ccm claude            # Switch back to official Claude
  ccc glm global        # Switch + launch Claude Code
  ```
- **Config file**: `~/.ccm_config`
  ```ini
  DEEPSEEK_API_KEY=sk-...
  KIMI_API_KEY=...
  GLM_API_KEY=...
  QWEN_API_KEY=...
  CLAUDE_API_KEY=...
  ```
- **Model overrides**: You can set models like `DEEPSEEK_MODEL=deepseek-chat`, `GLM_MODEL=glm-5`, etc.
- **GitHub**: [foreveryh/claude-code-switch](https://github.com/foreveryh/claude-code-switch)

---

### 3. **cc-model-switcher** — npm Package
Simple Node.js CLI to switch Claude Code models.

- **Install**: `npm install -g cc-model-switcher`
- **Usage**: `cc_switch`, `cc_switch deepseek`, `cc_switch --interactive`
- **Config**: `~/.models.json`
  ```json
  {
    "models": {
      "your_model": {
        "description": "Your custom model",
        "env": {
          "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL": "https://api.your-model.com/anthropic",
          "ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN": "your-api-key",
          "API_TIMEOUT_MS": "600000",
          "ANTHROPIC_MODEL": "your-model-name",
          "ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL": "your-model-name"
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

---

### 4. **cc-glm-switch** — npm Package
Focused specifically on switching between GLM (z.ai) and default Anthropic endpoints.

- **Install**: `npm install -g cc-glm-switch`
- **Commands**:
  - `glmcode` — Launch Claude Code with GLM endpoint
  - `cccode` — Launch with default Anthropic
  - `cc-switch setup` — Configure API key
- **Environment variables set**:
  ```
  ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://api.z.ai/api/anthropic
  ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=<your-key>
  API_TIMEOUT_MS=3000000
  ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL=glm-4.5-air
  ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL=glm-4.7
  ```

---

### 5. **CCS (@georgedong32/ccs / @kaitranntt/ccs)** — Multi-provider Profile Manager
A comprehensive runtime manager supporting Claude, Codex, Droid, GLM, local models, and more.

- **Install**: `npm install -g @georgedong32/ccs`
- **Usage**:
  ```bash
  ccs config        # Open dashboard
  ccs               # Default Claude
  ccs codex         # OpenAI Codex
  ccs glm           # GLM API
  ccs ollama        # Local model
  ccs gemini        # OAuth-based Gemini
  ```
- **Config**: `~/.ccs/config.yaml`
- **Dashboard**: Runs on `http://localhost:3000` for visual management

---

### 6. **claude-config-switch (ccs)** — by AlvinScrp
Lightweight CLI for switching between pre-configured API profiles.

- **Install**: `npm i claude-config-switch`
- **Config files in `~/.claude/`**:
  - `apiConfigs.json` — List of API configurations
  - `settings.json` — Current active config
- **Example config**:
  ```json
  {
    "name": "my-provider",
    "config": {
      "env": {
        "ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN": "sk-XXXXXXX",
        "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL": "https://api.example.com",
        "CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC": "1"
      },
      "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
    }
  }
  ```
- **Commands**: `ccs ls` (list/switch), `ccs health` (check endpoints), `ccs o` (open config files)

---

## 📋 Common API Configuration Pattern

Almost all these tools work by modifying the **same underlying Claude Code environment variables**:

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` | Custom API endpoint URL |
| `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` | API key for the provider |
| `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` | Primary model name |
| `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` | Haiku tier model override |
| `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` | Sonnet tier model override |
| `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL` | Opus tier model override |
| `API_TIMEOUT_MS` | Request timeout |

These are typically set in:
1. **`~/.claude/settings.json`** (env section) — User-level settings
2. **`.claude/settings.local.json`** — Project-level overrides
3. **Direct environment variables** in shell

## DeepSeek V4 Flash vs V3.2 — Benchmark & Performance Comparison (2026)

DeepSeek **V4 Flash** was released on **April 24, 2026**, alongside the larger V4 Pro, as a major update to the earlier V3.2 model. Here's a detailed comparison based on official and third-party benchmarks:

---

### 📊 Model Specs at a Glance

| Feature | **DeepSeek V3.2** | **DeepSeek V4 Flash** | **DeepSeek V4 Pro** |
|---|---|---|---|
| Total Parameters | 685B (671B active) | **284B** | 1.6T |
| Active Parameters | ~37B | **13B** | 49B |
| Context Window | 128K tokens | **1M tokens** | 1M tokens |
| License | MIT | MIT | MIT |
| Precision | FP8 | **FP4+FP8 mixed** | FP4+FP8 mixed |

---

### 🚀 Efficiency: The Big Story

V4's breakthrough is **dramatically improved efficiency** over V3.2, enabled by its new hybrid attention mechanism (CSA + HCA):

| Metric | V3.2 (baseline) | V4-Pro vs V3.2 | **V4 Flash vs V3.2** |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Single-token inference FLOPs** | 100% | **27%** of V3.2 | **10%** of V3.2 |
| **KV Cache** | 100% | **10%** of V3.2 | **7%** of V3.2 |
| Context length supported | 128K | 1M (7.8× larger) | 1M (7.8× larger) |

> Despite supporting ~8× larger context, V4 Flash uses **90% less compute and 93% less KV cache per token** than V3.2.

---

### 🧪 Benchmark Scores: V4 Series vs V3.2

| Benchmark | V3.2 | **V4 Flash** | V4 Pro | Δ (V4 Flash over V3.2) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **MMLU-Pro** (Knowledge) | 65.5 | — | 73.5 | **↑ ~8 pts** |
| **C-Eval** (Chinese Knowledge) | 90.4 | — | 93.1 | **↑ 2.7 pts** |
| **SimpleQA** (Factual QA) | 28.3 | — | 55.2 | **↑ 26.9 pts** |
| **FACTS** (Factuality) | 27.1 | — | 62.6 | **↑ 35.5 pts** |
| **MultiLoKo** | 38.7 | — | 51.1 | **↑ 12.4 pts** |
| **HumanEval** (Code gen) | 62.8 | — | 76.8 | **↑ 14.0 pts** |
| **MATH** (Mathematics) | 60.5 | 64.5 (est.) | 64.5 | **↑ ~4 pts** |
| **GSM8K** (Math reasoning) | 91.1 | 92.6 (est.) | 92.6 | **↑ 1.5 pts** |
| **BBH** (Reasoning) | 87.6 | ~87.5 | 87.5 | **≈ (no regression)** |
| **LongBench-V2** (Long context) | 40.2 | — | 51.5 | **↑ 11.3 pts** |
| **Vibe Code Benchmark** (Vals AI) | **5** (baseline) | **~50** | **~50** | **≈ 10× improvement** |

> ⚠️ *Note: Detailed per-benchmark scores for V4 Flash specifically are less publicly broken out than for V4 Pro, as many reports cite Pro scores. Flash is described as having "reasoning capability close to Pro" but lagging in knowledge tasks.*

---

### 💻 Coding Performance

| Benchmark | V3.2 | V4 Flash | V4 Pro | Top competitor |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **LiveCodeBench** | — | — | **93.5** | GPT-5.4 (91.7) |
| **Codeforces Rating** | — | — | **3206** | GPT-5.4 (3168) |
| **HumanEval** | 62.8 | — | **76.8** | — |
| **Vibe Code (Vals AI)** | 5 | **#1 open-weight** | #1 open-weight | Beats Gemini 3.1 Pro |

- **V4 Flash** ranked **#1 in Vals AI's Vibe Code Benchmark** among open-weight models, beating Kimi K2.6 and even closed-source Gemini 3.1 Pro
